decision.reason: BigQuery can express MERGE-based writes and hash functions, the adapter renders deterministic review artifacts, a compact opt-in real-account smoke passed first-run plus no-change replay for hash_diff_upsert, historical and snapshot_reconcile_soft_delete, a follow-up hash_diff_upsert smoke passed changed-row replay plus null/duplicate merge-key preflight failures, a historical edge smoke passed delete-expression expiration plus late-arriving reject failure evidence, and a snapshot variant smoke passed complete-source blocking, same-hash reactivation, missing-row tombstone and no-change replay. Production-sized benchmarks passed hash_diff_upsert initial load, no-change replay, changed-row wave, duplicate/null key failure evidence and BigQuery overlap serialization, plus historical initial/replay/change/delete/late-arriving cases and snapshot initial/replay/tombstone/reactivation cases. ContractForge has accepted hash_diff_upsert production parity using GCP, AWS, Snowflake and Fabric evidence; historical and snapshot production parity remains open.
decision.planner_behavior: Keep GCP planning at REVIEW_REQUIRED for hash_diff_upsert, historical and snapshot_reconcile_soft_delete. Render review artifacts and opt-in executable smoke SQL when enough contract metadata exists. Do not silently fall back to append, overwrite or current-state upsert.

required_before_promotion: Cross-adapter production-sized parity acceptance for historical and snapshot_reconcile_soft_delete.
Release decision that explicitly moves advanced write modes from review-required to stable-final for GCP.
Cost and cleanup retention policy for retained advanced-write benchmark tables.

validated_evidence.summary: Compact real-account BigQuery smokes passed first-run and no-change replay for hash_diff_upsert, historical and snapshot_reconcile_soft_delete through explicit --allow-review-required execution. Follow-up smokes passed hash_diff_upsert changed-row/null-key/duplicate-key cases, historical delete/late-arriving reject cases, snapshot complete-source reactivation/tombstone cases, a production-sized hash_diff_upsert benchmark with overlap serialization, and a production-sized historical/snapshot benchmark.

acceptance: Hash_diff_upsert production parity is accepted by GCP-BQ-12C. Stable-final GCP claims still exclude historical and snapshot soft-delete support until cross-adapter production parity is explicitly accepted; all three advanced modes have GCP production-sized review evidence.
